mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and compress forward earnings multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals impending recessionary pressures that force equity risk premiums higher and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in over 60% of triggered months or the signal shows zero predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in more than 50% of quarters or interaction term loses statistical significance at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates rising factory utilization and capacity constraints that boost near-term corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns during early-cycle periods or produces negative alpha after transaction costs.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation exceeding 3.0 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and forces negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold or the signal shows no predictive accuracy in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which drives consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or positive payroll growth correlates with negative equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with strong retail sales indicate synchronized consumer confidence and credit availability, which sustains corporate earnings growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction signal produces negative forward returns in over 55% of quarters or loses predictive power when either series declines.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising federal funds rates signals a tightening-induced supply contraction that erodes near-term profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns during late-cycle tightening periods or interaction term shows no statistical significance.
